category_title: Young children with anxiety exhibiting internalizing 'Freeze' or 'Flight' behaviors
student_description: This student has an inhibited temperament and struggles with emotional regulation, frequently exhibiting 'Freeze' or 'Flight' responses (withdrawing, crying, shaking, complaining of feeling unwell) when faced with uncertainty or transitions. They uniquely require highly predictable environments, explicit time boundaries, and concrete visual supports—for both emotional check-ins and schedules—to feel safe enough to engage.

criteria.rationale: Intolerance of uncertainty is a core trigger for this student, so repeatedly framing the exact time boundaries of the session builds long-term safety.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes a routine of explicitly announcing the session's exact duration at the beginning and consistently reminding the student of this timeframe.

criteria.rationale: Because transitions cause severe anxiety, the student requires visual schedules provided well before the transition occurs to mentally prepare.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates visual schedules or charts (detailing names, dates, and times) that are presented to the student well before any transition occurs.

criteria.rationale: The student's inhibited temperament requires sustained, close-proximity support to ensure they do not slip into a withdrawn 'shell' during activities.
criteria.check: The long-term plan ensures the student receives sustained, intensive individualized attention (e.g., small group ratios or continuous monitoring) to prevent them from withdrawing.
